page: Microsoft Azure D96pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache, with a single thread per core. The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It also includes 5669 GB of bundled local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals that the D96pds_v6 excels in multi-threaded and memory-intensive workloads. While single-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, multi-core execution is top-tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 198272.95 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 23821.0. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 10895.42 GB/sec for small block cached reads and 464.68 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. The server also demonstrates top-tier efficiency in database operations, achieving 170.16 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a top-tier PassMark database score.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the D96pds_v6 is driven by its high resource density and ARM-based architecture, which offers strong multi-threaded throughput per unit of power and resource cost. However, a notable tradeoff is the average single-core performance and weak text generation speeds for smaller LLM models, though it achieves top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large models. Given its massive 5669 GB local storage, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core capabilities, this server is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 198272.95 and 10895.42 GB/sec cached read bandwidth. While single-core performance remains average, its multi-threaded capabilities, high-speed database operations, and substantial local storage make it highly efficient for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 96 dedicated cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM-based server featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, massive local storage, and top-tier multi-threaded memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 cores operating at 3.4 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
5669 GB of bundled local NVMe storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM inference
